topics: Impulse and Step responses of first and second order systems- Pole dominance for higher order systems. Time domain specifications. Error analysis: Steady state error analysis and static error constants
Root locus technique: Construction of Root locus - stability analysis- effect of addition of poles and zeros

Effect of positive feedback systems on Root locus. Controller design: Types of controllers and their control action-proportional (P), integral (I), derivative (D), PID control. PID tuning using Ziegler-Nichols method
Bode Plot: Construction, Concept of gain margin and phase margin

correlation between time domain and frequency domain responses (Resonant peak and resonant frequency). Introduction to compensators. (Concept only). Polar plot: Gain margin and phase margin, Stability analysis. Nyquist stability criterion. Concept of Nichols Chart

Introduction to state-space modelling: State variables, state equations. State variable representation of electrical systems. Relationship between State space and Transfer function models: Derivation of transfer functions from state equations. Controllable, Observable and Diagonal/Jordan canonical forms. Introduction to similarity transformations (concept only). Solution of time invariant systems: Solution of time response of autonomous systems and forced systems. State transition matrix
computation using Method of Laplace Transform and Cayley Hamilton theorem. Controllability & Observability: Definition, Kalman’s test
